The Auditorium PapersThe ProofGate E

One hero, held up
against your board.

The engine now works from your board's own pixels and a craft-density scene library — not hand-drawn minimums. Below: the same Auditorium landing hero built two ways, then all three images side by side. You call whether the bar is met.

A

The board itself, re-inked

RECOMMENDED

Your arch-hall image, machine-mapped onto the brand palette (16 inks, posterized, grained) by the new image_lab tool — every shelf, document and the reading figure survive; the lamp stays the only glowing thing. This is how hero art gets made from any image you drop into the library.

A FREE, OPEN CATALOG OF 145 AUDIT BRIEFS

Every audit gets
a standing ovation.

Point your coding agent at any repo. Each brief runs the same four-phase audit and files one evidence-backed report — → BUGS.md — at your repo root.

145 BRIEFS · 37 PLAYBOOKS · ANY CODING AGENT · DOORS OPEN, NO SIGN-UP
B

Procedural, at full craft density

No photo source — the scene library drawing the same world: wobbled arch strokes, roughened gouache edges, two hundred hand-jittered archive documents, perspective checkerboard, hatched vault shadows, an ink figure for scale. For when a surface needs art and no reference exists.

Side by side

Gate E — is the bar met?

If A (or B) passes your squint test against the board, the site-wide application proceeds in this language — heroes from image_lab-processed art, secondary surfaces from the scene library, every rule from the new mind library (design-engine/library/). If it's still not right, say what's off — this gate repeats until it isn't. Note: drop ANY new reference images into library/boards/ and they become usable hero art the same day; if you ever provide an image-generation API key, generated art plugs into the exact same pipeline.

design engine · excellence upgrade proof · fonts embedded · art: image_lab (board re-ink) + scene.py (procedural) · no site surfaces changed
